vision_analysis.primary_subject: attic floor with blown-in cellulose insulation
vision_analysis.context_notes: Insulation appears to average 6-8 inches deep, settled in places.

input.transcript: Attic insulation is blown-in cellulose, averaging about 7 inches. That's around R-25. Current code is R-49 for this zone. Functional but undersized by today's standards.

expected.rationale: Under-insulated for current code but not a defect — pre-existing condition. Info finding documenting type and depth per SoP §3.9. Buyer's call whether to upgrade.

vision_analysis.primary_subject: attic ridge area with no visible vents

visible_defects.defect: No ridge vent or gable vents visible; soffit vents covered with insulation

input.transcript: Attic ventilation is poor. No ridge vent, soffits are blocked by insulation. There's some moisture staining starting. Need to open the soffits and probably add a ridge vent before this becomes mold.

expected.rationale: SoP requires reporting general absence of ventilation. Moisture staining present means it's progressing — minor_repair, not just info.
